string.text: Smart Balance dynamically adapts respawn to where combat actually happens. It counts deaths per region and faction, then advances the cooldown at the matching smart farthest from you so refills land off-screen. The engine still owns every spawn. Smart Balance only nudges a timestamp it was going to read anyway. Without it, vanilla's global fixed cooldown leaves fight regions empty while quiet maps overcrowd.
Master toggle for Smart Balance. When off, no death tracking and no cooldown advances.

How many combat bursts it takes to fully accelerate one smart terrain from full cooldown to the floor. A burst is one squad's worth of deaths in the affected faction (set automatically from the largest possible squad size for that faction). At 1, a single burst pushes a smart all the way to the floor. At 4 (default), four bursts step it through at 25% per burst. At 8, eight bursts at 12.5% per burst. Higher values pace acceleration across longer combat so a single firefight does not flush everything at once.

The minimum cooldown that must always remain on a smart terrain after Smart Balance applies an acceleration, in game-minutes. The engine ages the final wait out on its own clock and fires the respawn from there. A smart already within this margin of firing naturally is skipped. Default 120 minutes (two game-hours). Raise to leave more delay between combat and refill; lower for snappier turnover.

Inventory Balance trims excess loot from long-lived NPCs. A lightweight scanner walks online stalkers one per frame, releasing the surplus above per-category limits and rescanning each NPC at most once per game-day. Companions, story characters, traders, and named NPCs are never touched. Without it, every looted item fills one of the engine's ~65000 alife slots and saves drift toward crash.

Master toggle for Inventory Balance. The scanner walks the eligible online set in small batches spread across frames, rescanning each NPC at most once per game-day. Per-category limits load from configs/alifebalance/ab_inventory_policy.ltx (DLTX-overridable). Coverage: equipped-tier ammo (counted in rounds), grenades, consumables (medkit, bandage, antirad, stim, pill, food, drink), and gear (weapons, outfits, helmets, artefacts, crafting, devices). Quest items, equipped gear, story_id items, companion gifts, and player-strapped weapons are always preserved. Companions, story characters, traders, and named NPCs are filtered out at the scheduler and never scanned. When off, vanilla trim still applies but online inventories accumulate without bound.

How many NPCs the scanner trims per frame inside a cycle. The default of 1 keeps every frame cheap, with one inventory walk at a time. Raise only if the scanner struggles to keep up with the eligible set on very large saves. The per-NPC rescan cooldown already paces total work across long stretches of game-time, so this slider rarely needs changing.

An NPC is not rescanned until this many game-hours have passed since the last visit. Default 24 (one game-day). Lower values trim more aggressively against fast hoarders. Higher values reduce overall work and let NPCs hold a modest stash longer between visits. This cooldown is the rate limit on the whole feature.

Diagnostic tools for tuning and troubleshooting. Raise log level for structured tracing of every death, advance, eligibility scan, and trim cycle to alifebalance.log (small performance cost at DEBUG).

Restore all AlifeBalance MCM settings to factory defaults. Use after upgrading from a version with different MCM structure (the old settings remain as orphaned keys in the MCM storage file but no longer apply).
Warning: save or discard pending MCM changes first; otherwise they override the reset on the next Accept.

Controls verbosity of alifebalance.log. ERROR logs crashes only. WARN adds unexpected conditions. INFO adds startup, configuration, and reset events. DEBUG traces every death, advance, eligibility scan, gate outcome, and per-cycle summary. Default WARN is silent in normal play; DEBUG is for tuning or bug reports.

Drops a green PDA map marker on every smart terrain that receives a cooldown advance. The marker lingers for five real-time minutes and then clears itself. Right-click any marker to teleport to that smart or display its full advance history. Independent of log level, so you can run with markers on and logging off for a clean session view.
